2018 wheel range it consists of two lines arrow and performance and I'd really like to focus on the Arrow line so the DT Swiss aor wheel set is going to come in three depths 80 62 and 48 and in both rim and disc brake versions but what's really interesting about what DT Swiss have done with this wheel set is the way they've gone about designing them so they've defined two types of drag there's translational drag which I think is probably easiest to think of as pretty much anything that creates drag or resistance on your bikes that could be your position on the bike it could be your helmet it could be the frame it could be the handle bars whatever and then specific to Wheel sets there's rotational track now they reckon that around 25% of any Wheel sets drag does come from this rotational drag so they think they' have identified some seriously very very big ways to make their wheels significantly more Arrow they're not stopping here though they are promising further developments with this identification of rotational drag moving on from that the performance range is going to be working on improving their existing line of Wheel sets so there's Al going to be some very cool stuff there too moving on from DT Swiss Wheels to women's specific bikes Canyon have had a women's team for a couple of Seasons now arguably on a side note I think their women's team may have one of the coolest kits in either the men's or the women's pelaton but all the while they've been racing they have been Racing for the most part on unisex bikes but Canyon have been working very very hard behind the scenes to develop a women's specific range of bikes taking into account all the needs of female cyclists personally I had no idea of the depth of differences that went into men's bike design versus women's bike design but I was absolutely fascinated to find out and Canyon have made changes including 650b wheels which you might be familiar with if you're a mountain biker for so now we are creating a three extra small that's even one frame size smaller than we had before the reason is that women are actually smaller than men and we wanted to fit actually as many women as
